title: Hydrogen Bond
summary: The weak non-covalent attraction holding complementary base pairs together inside the structures/double-helix. A–T pairs share 2 H-bonds; G–C pairs share 3 H-bonds, so G–C-rich regions are slightly harder to separate. Hydrogen bonds are weak enough to be broken thermally — that's why heat (e.g. 95 °C in techniques/pcr|PCR denaturation) separates DNA strands without breaking the covalent backbone.
